oneOf.description:
The session's agent workflow exists but failed during bootstrap
(rehydration) and cannot serve runs. Distinct from `NotFound` (no
workflow) so clients/bridges treat it as a session recovery problem
rather than an ordinary "answer this message" failure.

AuthClientCreateParams.description:
Register an OAuth client configuration. `client_secret` is the second
deliberate inbound-plaintext path after `auth/grants/import`: it is
encrypted on receipt and never returned by any method. `Debug` output
redacts it; request logging must never echo these params.

authorizeUrl.description:
Authorization URL the user must open. It embeds the one-time `state`;
treat it as sensitive and do not log it server-side.

AuthGrantImportParams.description:
Import a static bearer credential as an auth grant. This is the one
deliberate inbound-plaintext path: `token` is encrypted on receipt and is
never returned by any method. `Debug` output redacts the token; request
logging must never echo these params.

metadata.description:
Non-secret provider-specific metadata (for GitHub App installation
grants: installation id, account, permissions, repository selection).

oneOf.description:
Stored model provider API key; the key arrives via `credential` and is
encrypted on receipt.

Bind an existing auth grant as a model provider credential. No
`credential` is accepted; the grant's tokens stay in the grant store.

AuthProviderConfigView.description:
Non-secret, provider-specific configuration. New providers add a
variant, not a table.

oneOf.description:
Stored model provider API key (`model:<provider_id>` rows). The key itself
is the provider credential and never appears in views.

OAuth-grant-backed model provider credential: provider calls send the
bound grant's access token as an OAuth bearer token.

AuthProviderCreateParams.description:
Register an auth provider. `credential` (for GitHub Apps: the private
key PEM) is the third deliberate inbound-plaintext path: it is encrypted
on receipt and never returned by any method. `Debug` output redacts it;
request logging must never echo these params.

BlobPutParams.description:
`blobs/put` is batch-native: pass one item to store a single blob. Results
come back in request order.

key.description:
Stable client-chosen context key. Re-sending the same key with the
same content is a no-op, so the key doubles as the idempotency handle.

activationTextTruncated.description:
True when `activation_text` was cut off at the server-side length cap.
The committed context entry always holds the full text.

ContextEntryView.description:
A session context entry, faithful to the stored engine entry: keyed,
kind-tagged, ref-backed. Keys are a stable extension point — clients
reconstruct derived surfaces (e.g. the prompted instruction set via the
`prompt_instructions/` key prefix) by filtering on `key` and fetching
bodies through `blobs/read`. `text` inlines blob content only for
message and tool entries; every other kind is ref + preview.

keys.description:
Active context keys to remove. Removing a key that is already absent
is a per-key no-op (`absent`), so retries are idempotent. Keys under
reserved runtime namespaces (`run.`) are rejected request-level.

EnvironmentsFeature.description:
Grants attaching/activating session environments and their process/job
tool surface.

providers.description: Absent means every registered provider is allowed.

FeaturesConfig.description:
Capability grants. An absent feature is not granted; `{}` grants it with
defaults. Every block carries a behavior `version` that pins semantics.

FleetFeature.description:
Grants the Fleet subagent control plane
(agent_spawn/send/read/list/cancel and profile_list/read).

allow.description: Absent means all named profiles are visible/readable/spawnable.

bases.description: Absent means all bases are allowed.

GenerationConfig.description:
Turn-shaping defaults applied to every LLM generation. Per-run overrides
ride `session/runs/start`.

parallelToolUse.description:
Whether the model may call several tools in one turn; absent leaves
the provider default.

reasoningEffort.description:
Reasoning effort tier as a provider-native string (e.g. "none",
"high", "xhigh", "max"); validated against the session's provider.

permissions.description: Fine-grained permission map as GitHub reports it.

LimitsConfig.description: Run budget defaults enforced by the engine drive loop.

McpFeature.description:
Grants remote MCP tools by declaring linked servers from the universe MCP
catalog; must link at least one server, with unique server ids.

McpServerInput.description: Full MCP server document as submitted by clients.

McpServerLink.description:
A linked catalog server with optional per-session overrides; absent
fields defer to the catalog record's defaults.

authGrantId.description:
Universe-scoped auth grant used to authenticate against the server;
compatibility with the server's auth policy is validated at put time.

expectedRevision.description:
Checked only when the server already exists; absent replaces (or
creates) unconditionally.

MessagingFeature.description:
Grants the messaging toolset (message_send/react/edit/noop) for sessions
bound to a chat channel.

parallelToolUse.description: Omitted when the provider model-list API does not report this fact.

reasoningEfforts.description:
Provider-reported effort values. Omitted when the provider does not
report per-model effort support.

ModelListParams.description:
Direct provider model discovery. Each invocation asks the supported
provider APIs again; clients refresh by calling this method.

selectableOnly.description:
Apply Lightspeed's small, conservative selectable-model policy. It
removes OpenAI model-id families that are clearly not text-generation
routes (embeddings, moderation, image/video, speech, and realtime).
It is an ID policy, not a provider capability claim.

ModelProviderDiscoveryView.description:
Best-effort status for one provider. A provider failure does not discard
models discovered from other providers.

error.description:
Sanitized Lightspeed error; never a credential, request header, or raw
upstream response body.

ModelView.description: A model route returned directly by a provider API.

apiKind.description: Canonical Lightspeed provider API kind, e.g. `openai:responses`.

source.description: Always `provider` in P97: there is no maintained model catalog.

displayName.description: Human-readable purpose shown in key-management interfaces.

principal.description:
Audit principal applied to grants and flows created through this key.
This does not grant platform/operator authority.

OperatorApiKeyCreateResponse.description:
A newly minted key. `secret` is returned only by create and cannot be
recovered later. Its custom `Debug` implementation redacts the DTO before
JSON-RPC serialization; the serialized response payload remains sensitive
and must not be logged.

OperatorApiKeyView.description:
Non-secret API-key metadata. The owning universe is supplied by every
request and intentionally omitted from entries so list responses cannot
become a deployment-wide tenant catalog by accident.

OperatorOutboundMessageView.description:
One pending outbox entry with its owning universe. Acknowledge through
the per-universe `outbox/ack` (with the entry's universe header); the
global cursor only drives reading.

OperatorOutboxReadParams.description:
Multiplexed outbox read: one long-poll serves every universe of the
deployment, replacing one `outbox/read` tailer per universe. `seq` is a
deployment-global cursor (the outbox sequence is one identity column
across universes), so a single `after` resumes the whole stream.

after.description:
Return pending entries with `seq` greater than this cursor. Restart
from 0 to re-read undelivered entries after a consumer restart.

waitMs.description: Long-poll wait in milliseconds when no entries are pending.

nextAfter.description: Cursor to pass as `after` on the next read.

created.description: False when the universe already existed (create is idempotent).

OperatorUniverseDeleteResponse.description:
Purge report. The purge is idempotent: rerunning after a partial failure
resumes where it stopped, and the universe row is deleted last so a
half-purged universe is still visible to `operator/universes/read`.

blobObjectsDeleted.description: External object-store blobs deleted during the purge.

workflowsTerminated.description: Live session workflows terminated during the purge.

OperatorUniverseView.description:
Per-universe stats view. Counts are cheap aggregates computed at read
time, not maintained counters — approximate under concurrent writes.

lastActivityAtMs.description:
Most recent session activity (`max(sessions.updated_at_ms)`); absent
when the universe has no sessions.

after.description:
Return pending entries with `seq` greater than this cursor. Restart
from 0 to re-read undelivered entries after a consumer restart.

waitMs.description: Long-poll wait in milliseconds when no entries are pending.

nextAfter.description: Cursor to pass as `after` on the next read.

expectedRevision.description:
Checked only when the profile already exists; absent replaces (or
creates) unconditionally.

submissionId.description:
Client-supplied idempotency key, unique per session. Retrying
`session/runs/start` with the same submission id and the same
source/config returns the original run instead of starting a second
one; reusing a submission id with different source or config is
rejected.

force.description:
Cancel the active run and drop queued runs instead of rejecting on
active work. Recovers sessions whose workflow no longer exists (e.g.
after an operator terminate) by reconciling the session log directly.

SessionConfig.description:
Declared session configuration document.

Sparse and capability-oriented: an omitted section means defaults, an
absent feature is not granted (no tools, no access). The document is
replaced whole via `session/config/put`; reads return exactly the stored
document, so read-modify-write round-trips.

model.description:
Absent on input means the deployment default model. Documents read
back from a session always carry the model; the provider api kind is
pinned for the session's lifetime.

SessionConfigPutParams.description:
Replace the session config with a complete document. Anything omitted
from the document reverts to defaults; an absent feature is revoked.
Requires an idle session; putting an identical document is a no-op that
leaves the revision untouched.

expectedConfigRevision.description:
Checked against the session's current config revision when present;
absent replaces unconditionally.

waitMs.description:
Long-poll: when no events exist past `after`, hold the request until
one lands or this many milliseconds elapse, then return a normal
(possibly empty) page. Zero or absent preserves immediate return.
Values above the server cap are clamped, not rejected.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's `nextCursor`.

nextCursor.description:
Present when more sessions exist past this page. Ordering is most
recently updated first; pages can drift under concurrent activity.

displayName.description: New display name; absent clears it.

config.description:
The stored sparse config document, exactly as last put (model and
feature versions materialized at admission). Effective tool reality
is visible via `active_tools`.

TimersFeature.description:
Grants timer promises through the sleep tool plus the base concurrency
tools (await/cancel/detach).

VfsFeature.description:
Grants the session virtual filesystem: mounts may be attached and the VFS
catalog is surfaced. Sub-grants are independent; `{}` grants a VFS with
no tools and no sourcing.

skills.description: Skill discovery sourcing from the VFS.

tools.description:
Agent-facing filesystem tool surface; absent = no fs tools. Per-path
writability is defined by each mount's own access.

roots.description:
Absent means the conventional roots; an explicit list must be
non-empty.

Absent means the conventional roots; an explicit list must be
non-empty.

snapshotRef.description:
Snapshot to seed the workspace from. Absent starts the workspace from
the empty snapshot, committed server-side.

bytes.description: Total byte size of the head snapshot.

files.description: File count of the head snapshot.

WebFeature.description:
Grants network access through the web toolset; `fetch` and `search` are
independently granted, and a web block granting neither is rejected.

allowedDomains.description: Absent means all domains; an explicit list must be non-empty.

info.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST /rpc. Results are wrapped in the AgentApiOutcome envelope; session events are consumed via session/events/read cursor pagination.
info.title: Lightspeed Agent API

methods.description: Returns protocol version, server identity, and supported capabilities without changing universe state.
methods.name: initialize

methods.description: Creates a session with optional config/profile setup. Retrying an existing session id returns that session; creation settings apply only when it is first created.
methods.name: session/start

methods.description: Returns the current projected session, including sparse config and revisions, lifecycle/run state, active context, and derived tools.
methods.name: session/read

methods.description: Returns a cursor-paginated summary list ordered by most recent update. Pages may shift while sessions are changing.
methods.name: session/list

methods.description: Replaces the complete sparse config while the session is idle. Use the current config revision for safe read-modify-write; omitted features are revoked and an identical document is a no-op.
methods.name: session/config/put

methods.description: Sets the display name, or clears it when displayName is omitted.
methods.name: session/rename

methods.description: Closes an idle session and detaches its environment bindings. Force mode cancels active work, drops queued runs, and can recover a session whose workflow is unavailable.
methods.name: session/close

methods.description: Permanently removes session storage after the session has been closed; close active/open sessions first.
methods.name: session/delete

methods.description: Reads events after a cursor and optionally long-polls when caught up. Continue from nextCursor/headCursor and inspect complete/gap rather than assuming an uninterrupted page.
methods.name: session/events/read

methods.description: Admits a batch of context entries with per-entry results. Stable keys make same-content retries no-ops; media preprocessing can fail one entry without discarding successful entries.
methods.name: session/context/append

methods.description: Removes active entries by stable key with per-key results. Missing keys are idempotent no-ops; runtime-reserved run keys cannot be removed.
methods.name: session/context/remove

methods.description: Runs the configured compaction policy on an open idle session and waits for the resulting context revision.
methods.name: session/context/compact

methods.description: Accepts input or existing context keys and returns once the run is queued/accepted, not when it finishes. Supply submissionId for retry safety, then follow session events or reread the session.
methods.name: session/runs/start

methods.description: Requests cancellation of the named queued or active run and returns its current projected state; observe session events for terminal completion.
methods.name: session/runs/cancel

methods.description: Refreshes the session's configured VFS skill catalog and reports which discovered skills are enabled and active. An absent catalog yields an empty result.
methods.name: session/skills/list

methods.description: Returns skill instructions currently injected into context, including activation scope and source.
methods.name: session/skills/active

methods.description: Loads an enabled skill from the current catalog and injects its instructions into an open idle session. Run-scoped activation is the default.
methods.name: session/skills/activate

methods.description: Removes an active skill's injected context from an open idle session; the skill must currently be active.
methods.name: session/skills/deactivate

methods.description: Applies a named or inline profile's config, instructions, mounts, and environment setup to an existing session; mutating profile sections require it to be open and idle. Pass current revisions to guard concurrent changes.
methods.name: session/profiles/apply
methods.summary: Apply a profile to a session

methods.description: Binds a snapshot or workspace at a path on an open idle session that grants VFS. Workspace mounts follow that workspace's current head.
methods.name: session/mounts/put
methods.summary: Create or replace a session mount

methods.description: Returns the session's snapshot/workspace bindings and access modes.
methods.name: session/mounts/list

methods.description: Removes a binding from an open idle session without deleting its source snapshot or workspace.
methods.name: session/mounts/delete

methods.description: Returns one session-local environment alias joined with current instance/provider availability and activation state.
methods.name: session/environments/read

methods.description: Returns all environment aliases attached to the session and identifies the active tool target, if any.
methods.name: session/environments/list

methods.description: Binds an existing universe environment instance under a session-local alias, optionally activating it. The session must grant environments and allow the provider.
methods.name: session/environments/attach
methods.summary: Attach an environment to a session

methods.description: Selects an attached, available environment as the process/filesystem tool target while the session is idle.
methods.name: session/environments/activate

methods.description: Clears the active environment tool target without detaching any binding or closing the underlying instance.
methods.name: session/environments/deactivate

methods.description: Detaches the session-local binding; detaching the active target requires an idle session and deactivates it first. The universe instance and jobs remain independently owned.
methods.name: session/environments/detach

methods.description: Maps an environment variable name to an existing grant/provider/direct-secret handle for one session binding. The response exposes only the source handle, never secret material.
methods.name: session/environments/credentials/bind
methods.summary: Bind a credential into an environment

methods.description: Returns variable names and credential source handles for a session environment; resolved secret values are never returned.
methods.name: session/environments/credentials/list

methods.description: Removes one variable-to-credential mapping without deleting the underlying grant, provider credential, or secret.
methods.name: session/environments/credentials/unbind

methods.description: Asks a live provider with create capability to create a universe-owned environment instance. This does not attach the instance to any session.
methods.name: environments/create

methods.description: Returns the universe resource with its provider identity, current observed lifecycle, connection, scope, and capabilities.
methods.name: environments/read

methods.description: Lists universe-owned instances, optionally filtered by provider or observed target status.
methods.name: environments/list

methods.description: Tears down the universe resource through its provider. Closing is rejected while session bindings or nonterminal jobs still occupy the instance.
methods.name: environments/close

methods.description: Starts a dependency-aware job group on one environment instance. requestId is the retry identity; jobs are owned by the instance rather than a session.
methods.name: environments/jobs/create

methods.description: Reads selected job handles with bounded output, optional sequence continuation, and optional artifacts; use returned status/sequence data for polling.
methods.name: environments/jobs/read

methods.description: Lists durable job records across the universe, optionally narrowed to an instance or job group.
methods.name: environments/jobs/list

methods.description: Requests cancellation for selected jobs, optionally including dependents. Force is provider-specific escalation; inspect each per-job result.
methods.name: environments/jobs/cancel

methods.description: Queries supported providers directly on every call and returns best-effort selectable routes. One provider failure does not discard successful results from others.
methods.name: models/list

methods.description: Creates a new universe-scoped reusable profile document; use profiles/put for create-or-replace revision semantics.
methods.name: profiles/create

methods.description: Returns the complete profile document and current revision.
methods.name: profiles/read

methods.description: Returns lightweight summaries of universe-scoped reusable profiles.
methods.name: profiles/list

methods.description: Stores the complete profile document. Use expectedRevision from profiles/read when replacing to prevent lost updates; absence writes unconditionally.
methods.name: profiles/put
methods.summary: Create or replace an agent profile

methods.description: Deletes the catalog document; sessions previously created or configured from it retain their materialized state.
methods.name: profiles/delete

methods.description: Decodes and stores a batch of base64 payloads, returning immutable content references in request order. Re-uploading identical bytes is naturally deduplicated.
methods.name: blobs/put

methods.description: Returns the complete immutable blob as base64; large values count against gateway and MCP response limits.
methods.name: blobs/read

methods.description: Checks a batch of content references without returning blob bodies, preserving request order.
methods.name: blobs/has

methods.description: Validates and stores an immutable filesystem manifest. Upload referenced file blobs first; the returned snapshot ref is content-addressed.
methods.name: vfs/snapshots/commit

methods.description: Returns an immutable snapshot manifest and aggregate file/byte counts; file bodies remain separate blobs.
methods.name: vfs/snapshots/read

methods.description: Creates a universe workspace at an optional seed snapshot; absence starts from a server-created empty snapshot.
methods.name: vfs/workspaces/create

methods.description: Returns workspace metadata, current head snapshot, and revision for safe updates.
methods.name: vfs/workspaces/read

methods.description: Lists mutable universe workspaces with head snapshots, sizes, and revisions.
methods.name: vfs/workspaces/list

methods.description: Moves the workspace head to an existing snapshot and updates its display name. Pass expectedRevision from a read to prevent lost updates.
methods.name: vfs/workspaces/update

methods.description: Deletes the mutable workspace record; immutable snapshots and blobs remain content-addressed resources.
methods.name: vfs/workspaces/delete

methods.description: Stores the complete universe catalog document. Use expectedRevision when replacing; authenticated policies reference grants but never embed credentials.
methods.name: mcp/servers/put
methods.summary: Create or replace an MCP server record

methods.description: Returns one catalog document with defaults, auth policy, status, and revision; no credential value is exposed.
methods.name: mcp/servers/read

methods.description: Lists universe catalog entries, optionally filtered by lifecycle/configuration status.
methods.name: mcp/servers/list

methods.description: Deletes the catalog document. Existing session configs that reference it are not silently rewritten and may need explicit reconfiguration.
methods.name: mcp/servers/delete

methods.description: Publishes a controller endpoint, capabilities, implementation identity, and liveness lease. Intended for trusted provider/bridge infrastructure, not ordinary configuration clients.
methods.name: environments/providers/register

methods.description: Renews a provider lease and records its complete observed target descriptors. Omitted provided targets may become unknown; intended for provider infrastructure.
methods.name: environments/providers/heartbeat

methods.description: Marks provider presence offline without deleting its durable environment instance records.
methods.name: environments/providers/unregister

methods.description: Lists current provider presence with lease-derived online/stale/offline status, optionally filtered by status or kind.
methods.name: environments/providers/list

methods.description: Accepts a plaintext token, encrypts it immediately, and returns only grant metadata/token-presence flags. The token can never be read back through the API.
methods.name: auth/grants/import

methods.description: Returns principal, provider binding, scopes, audience, expiry, status, and token-presence flags; access and refresh token values are never returned.
methods.name: auth/grants/read

methods.description: Lists non-secret grant metadata for the universe, optionally filtered by status.
methods.name: auth/grants/list

methods.description: Marks the grant unusable by token consumers while retaining non-secret audit metadata.
methods.name: auth/grants/revoke

methods.description: Stores provider endpoints and client identity; an optional plaintext client secret is encrypted and represented thereafter only by hasClientSecret.
methods.name: auth/clients/create

methods.description: Returns endpoints, public client identity, defaults, and secret-presence state; the client secret is never returned.
methods.name: auth/clients/read

methods.description: Lists non-secret OAuth client registrations in the universe.
methods.name: auth/clients/list

methods.description: Deletes the client registration and its stored client secret; grants already created from it remain separate records.
methods.name: auth/clients/delete

methods.description: Creates a short-lived PKCE flow and returns a browser authorization URL containing one-time state. Treat the URL as sensitive and poll auth/flows/read for completion.
methods.name: auth/flows/start

methods.description: Polls a flow's pending/completed/failed/expired state and returns the resulting grant id when authorization succeeds; no token value is exposed.
methods.name: auth/flows/read

methods.description: Creates a model or GitHub credential source. Plaintext API keys/private keys are encrypted on receipt and later represented only by configuration plus hasCredential.
methods.name: auth/providers/create

methods.description: Returns provider kind, non-secret configuration, credential-presence state, and status; stored credentials are never returned.
methods.name: auth/providers/read

methods.description: Lists non-secret model/GitHub provider registrations for the universe.
methods.name: auth/providers/list

methods.description: Deletes the provider registration and its directly stored credential; separately stored grants remain independent records.
methods.name: auth/providers/delete

methods.description: Uses the registered GitHub App provider credential to query accessible installations and returns account/permission metadata without tokens.
methods.name: auth/github/installations/list

methods.description: Creates or refreshes a universe auth grant for one accessible installation. The installation token is brokered internally and never returned.
methods.name: auth/github/installations/grant
methods.summary: Grant access to a GitHub App installation

methods.description: Cursor-reads or long-polls the universe delivery outbox. Advance with nextAfter, but only outbox/ack marks individual entries delivered or failed.
methods.name: outbox/read

methods.description: Records delivered or failed delivery for one outbox entry and updates attempt/status state. Intended for messaging delivery workers.
methods.name: outbox/ack

methods.description: Creates the deployment tenant boundary for an explicit UUID. The operation is idempotent and reports whether a new universe was created.
methods.name: operator/universes/create

methods.description: Returns deployment-wide universe summaries with approximate live aggregate counts and last session activity.
methods.name: operator/universes/list

methods.description: Returns one deployment tenant summary with aggregate session, workspace, profile, and blob usage.
methods.name: operator/universes/read

methods.description: Permanently terminates live session workflows, deletes external blob objects, and cascades universe data. The purge is resumable/idempotent after partial failure.
methods.name: operator/universes/delete

methods.description: Mints an inbound gateway key for one existing universe. The plaintext secret is returned exactly once and cannot be recovered; persist only the displayed prefix for identification.
methods.name: operator/api-keys/create

methods.description: Returns only non-secret key metadata for the requested universe, including revocation and last-use timestamps. Plaintext secrets are never stored or returned.
methods.name: operator/api-keys/list

methods.description: Immediately and idempotently revokes the matching key only when it belongs to the requested universe. Unknown and foreign-universe prefixes return not found.
methods.name: operator/api-keys/revoke

methods.description: Cursor-reads or long-polls pending messages across all universes. Entries identify their universe; acknowledge each through universe-scoped outbox/ack.
methods.name: operator/outbox/read
